hypothesis: Adding one verifiable Northern Ontario-specific contextual detail per CMI body section — beyond the lede fact — will push draft quality from 9/10 to 10/10. Evidence: four formats (blog exp_3, FAQ exp_8, prose service exp_9, OEM brand/dealer exp_10) all hit 9/10 ceiling with same rules. Consistent gap across all: body sections 2-4 are proof-strong (named staff, service timelines) but local-light after the opening. Lede credibility comes from geographic concreteness — same principle applied per-section should create local depth throughout, not only at the entry point. Surface change: add per-section NO detail requirement to body structure rules. Test on synthetic surface (fictional Northern Ontario auto shop) to avoid client surface dependency and test industry generalisation.
changes_description: Added per-section NO-specific detail requirement to surface rules (Experiment 11 addition): each CMI body section must contain at least one verifiable Northern Ontario-specific detail — not just named staff and timelines. Synthetic output: fictional Northern Ontario auto shop (Shield Automotive, Chapleau ON) — winter tire service, prose page format. Industry: automotive (non-marine). Per-section NO details applied: lede=Chapleau first frost date (Oct 1 avg, 4-6wk ahead of GTA), pain=Oct 15 7°C threshold + Thanksgiving booking pattern, solution=Sudbury distributor 3-day lead, objection=Chapleau temperature range minus 35 to plus 30, CTA=Woodlands Road corridor + White River named routes. Named staff (Marcus/Dylan) in applicable sections. No em-dashes (verified). Synthetic content — not a Reyco deliverable. Output: experiments/outputs/exp_1778647664_m5ir7-winter-tire-service.md.
